attack_claim: Strong external inflows and macro resilience mean the squeeze thesis is overstated.
strongest_support: The synthesis recognizes strong external inflows and macro resilience.
failure_point: Macro resilience does not establish household comfort, informal-worker income offset, or household-wide income offset.

attack_claim: Formal TSS wage-food offset shows workers were cushioned.
strongest_support: The formal TSS wage-food offset is supported.
failure_point: The supported channel is formal-only and cannot be generalized to informal workers or all households.
boundary: Formal channel allowed; household-wide comfort forbidden.

attack_claim: Positive 2026 labor context means current income pressure was offset.
strongest_support: Official 2026 labor context is supported as current labor-market context.
failure_point: Labor/employment context does not lock a current income/remuneration metric.
boundary: Current labor context proving income offset remains REJECTED_BOUNDARY.

attack_claim: Because direct trade-down was not proven, households may not be under real pressure.
strongest_support: Direct current household trade-down remains NOT_PROVEN.
failure_point: Failure to prove trade-down is not proof of no pressure and not proof of comfort.
boundary: Households did not trade down also remains NOT_PROVEN.

attack_claim: Food pressure and tight household margins imply broad household collapse.
strongest_support: The synthesis recognizes food pressure and tight household margins.
failure_point: Pressure does not prove universal collapse, direct trade-down, or household-wide inability to offset.

strongest_support: Business pressure through MIPYME financing costs, repayment stress, and FX exposure is part of the synthesis.
failure_point: Business stress is a pressure channel, not direct proof of household-wide collapse or household trade-down.
boundary: Fiscal, credit, business, or macro proxies proving trade-down remains REJECTED_BOUNDARY.

attack_claim: Macro consumption/demand softness means households are broadly failing.
strongest_support: Household-side macro consumption/demand softness is included in the integrated thesis.
failure_point: Macro softness cannot identify household-wide income offset, informal income, or direct substitution behavior by itself.
boundary: Selective squeeze remains stronger than collapse.

attack_claim: Near-current 2025 FMCG behavior context proves trade-down and therefore collapse.
strongest_support: Near-current 2025 FMCG behavior context is supported for context.
failure_point: The 2025 context cannot prove current 2026 trade-down and cannot carry a collapse claim.
